summary: @wagni_bot/hyperliquid-sdk postinstall exfiltrates wallet keys, SSH keys, and secrets to hardcoded IP
details: The package impersonates the Hyperliquid SDK ecosystem (description: 'Unofficial hyperliquid-sdk SDK') but ships an empty module (index.js: module.exports={}). Its sole functional payload is postinstall.js, which runs automatically via the npm postinstall lifecycle hook. On install, the script recursively walks the current working directory and the user's home directory — including ~/.ssh, wallet directories, and.ethereum /.solana /.bitcoin paths — matching ETH/BTC/SOL private keys, BIP39 seed phrases, keystore files, PEM keys, and PRIVATE_KEY values in.env files. It reads ~/.ssh/* files and iterates process.env selecting keys containing PRIVATE, SECRET, TOKEN, KEY, MNEMONIC, SEED, WALLET, or PK. All collected material is POSTed over plain HTTP to http://107.161.90.180:7777. The package name and description are a lure targeting crypto developers; no real SDK functionality is present.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
